title: The Walter Morgan Meeting
content:
I was 21 years old. Senior at Princeton. No connections. No money. Just a scholarship and a thesis.

Walter Morgan—founder of Wellington Fund, a titan of the industry—somehow got hold of my thesis. He read it.

I sat across from this legend, sweating through my suit. My shoes were worn. I had to borrow a tie.

Then he said: 'Bogle, this is the best thesis I've ever read.'

He hired me on the spot.

The lesson? Do the work. Do the best work you possibly can. The right people will notice.
